description_long: This is a medium-sized, irregularly shaped rock with a predominantly white surface interspersed with gray and black mineral flecks, giving it a speckled granite-like appearance. The rock's surface is rough and uneven, with jagged edges and a somewhat triangular profile when viewed from certain angles. Some areas show subtle brownish discoloration, possibly from soil or weathering. The rock appears dense and solid, with no visible cavities or openings. There are faint, dark markings on one side, which may be numbers or letters, suggesting prior handling or cataloging. Its overall form and coloration suggest it could serve as a natural barrier or decorative element in an urban park or landscaped area.
description: A rough, speckled white and gray rock with jagged edges and some dark markings, resembling granite. Its irregular shape and solid appearance make it suitable as a natural barrier or landscape feature.
description_view_0: This view shows the rock's broadest face, highlighting its speckled white and gray coloration and rough, uneven surface.
description_view_1: From this angle, the rock appears more triangular, with a pointed end and visible dark markings on the lower left edge.
description_view_2: This side reveals a more blocky profile, with the speckled pattern continuing and the dark markings partially visible.
description_view_3: Here, the rock's surface appears more weathered and brownish, with a vertical edge and less visible speckling.
attribute_location: urban park or landscaped roadside area
